title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On December 26th, Ellery Queen and his father, Inspector Quin, drive a secondhand Duesenberg from a police meeting in Chicago toward the West Virginia village of Aroo. Ellery is driven by interest in a bizarre crime dubbed the "T-shaped murder." The victim, 46-year-old Andrew Van, the eccentric principal of Aroo, was decapitated and nailed to a T-shaped signpost at a T-shaped crossroads on Christmas morning. The crime is characterized by a recurring T-motif, including a T-shaped mark found on the door of Van's house.

At the crime scene, Ellery observes the gruesome details of the execution: four-inch nails were driven through Van's palms, armpits, and ankles to fix his body to the six-foot-tall signpost. The signpost is covered in dried blood. While the local police are baffled, Hampshire County District Attorney Crummett suspects a maniacal killer, noting that Van's only friend, a servant named Clinton, has also disappeared.

Despite Inspector Quin's frustration and desire to return to New York, Ellery is invigorated by the case. They drive into the dreary residential area of Aroo, passing a general store and a car repair shop. Ellery stops the car in front of the Arroyo Village Hall, a shoddy wooden structure, to locate the local sheriff. They find the official, identified by a sign as the Town Marshal, asleep at a desk inside the building.
